hero_subtitle: SFTY consolidates multiple KYT provider signals into a single operational layer to streamline wallet risk aggregation, case workflows, and audit evidence for digital asset compliance teams.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: KYT outputs remain separated across providers
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle with inconsistent wallet risk and transaction monitoring results when KYT data arrives from multiple disconnected providers. This separation creates manual reconciliation, weaker risk visibility and challenges in building a unified audit trail for digital asset transactions.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY centralizes wallet risk signals, transaction alerts and provider outputs into a unified workflow so compliance teams managing digital assets can review risk consistently with end-to-end audit evidence.
workflow_title: From KYT provider outputs to cases
workflow_description: SFTY aggregates and normalizes KYT provider signals, applies risk rules, routes reviews, opens cases, records decisions and preserves evidence within a structured compliance workflow.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. One audit-ready layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides VASPs a provider-neutral compliance layer that connects KYT outputs into structured workflows, cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence without replacing existing tools.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs by unifying KYT data, preserving audit-ready evidence and enabling scalable deployment with controlled governance and bank due diligence workflows.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how KYT providers integrate into one layer
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how SFTY connects wallet risk and transaction alerts
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit-ready evidence is preserved from KYT providers
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe provider orchestration models supporting multi-vendor KYT workflows
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for KYT provider integration?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects multiple KYT providers into one operational layer, aggregating wallet and transaction risk data for VASPs to improve compliance visibility and streamline workflows.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY connect KYT provider outputs?
faq_2_answer: SFTY normalizes and aggregates KYT wallet risk signals and transaction alerts, linking them to customers, counterparties and cases in a connected review and decision workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve KYT audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case history and supporting KYT evidence to support audit-ready records for traceability and regulatory review across wallet and transaction monitoring.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates with existing KYC, KYB, Travel Rule and screening providers, connecting their outputs into one structured compliance operating environment without requiring provider replacement.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYT or blockchain providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace KYT or blockchain analytics providers but complements them by turning provider outputs into structured workflows, cases, evidence and reports.
final_title: Connect KYT providers into one integration layer
final_description: Review how SFTY consolidates wallet risk signals, structures alert triage, preserves audit evidence and supports VASP compliance workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
